questions.text: What allows all adult citizens to vote regardless of their wealth or religion?
questions.explanation: Universal Adult Franchise is a key aspect of democratic societies based on equality.

questions.text: The Midday Meal scheme was first introduced in which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu was the first state in India to start this scheme in 2001.

questions.text: Who is the author of the autobiography 'Joothan'?
questions.explanation: Omprakash Valmiki wrote about his experiences as a Dalit boy in this book.

questions.text: Which article of the Indian Constitution prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, or caste?
questions.explanation: Article 15 ensures equality of access to public places and prohibits state discrimination.

questions.text: Rosa Parks was an African-American woman who started a protest in:
questions.explanation: Her refusal to give up her seat on a bus led to the Civil Rights Movement in the USA.

questions.text: The Civil Rights Act of 1964 prohibited discrimination in the USA based on:
questions.explanation: This landmark act ended segregation in public places in the United States.

options: Thinking of oneself and others as worthy of respect
questions.explanation: When people are treated unequally, their dignity is violated.

questions.text: Equality is a central feature of which form of government?
questions.explanation: Democracy thrives on the principle of equal rights for every individual.

questions.text: What is one of the main reasons why people are treated unequally in India?
questions.explanation: Caste, along with religion and gender, remains a major basis of inequality.

questions.text: The Indian Constitution recognizes every person as:
questions.explanation: The Constitution of India considers every individual, male or female, equal before the law.
